meta.title: Sapporo Kokusai Ski Resort
meta.description: Japow at the center—linking Sapporo, Jozankei, and Otaru.

hero.title:
Sapporo Kokusai
Ski Resort
hero.tagline: Japow at the hub of three worlds.

triangle.title:
City, onsen, port—
one snow links all three.
triangle.lead: Sapporo Kokusai is more than a ski hill: a geographic hub tying Sapporo (urban convenience and gastronomy), Jozankei (national-park nature and hot-spring culture), and Otaru (heritage architecture and maritime culture). Ski as your anchor and blend three distinct experiences seamlessly.
triangle.noticeBody: Otaru–Jozankei Route 1 (Asari Pass) is closed nights Nov–Apr, 19:00–07:00. "Otaru sushi at night, then Jozankei lodge" is impossible—plan daytime circulation and early dinners.
triangle.imgAlt: Jozankei snowscape and slopes—the regional hub

primary.title: World-class Japow, 60 min from the city
primary.lead: Light powder from the 1,100 m summit zone. Direct lines from New Chitose, then Sapporo soup curry, Otaru sushi, Jozankei snow-view baths—the core of a rare high-value regional package.

secondary.title: Onsen design that avoids culture clash
secondary.lead: In-room baths, private rentals, tattoo-friendly trials—raising area-wide standards for privacy and inclusion for Western guests and diverse dietary needs.

mobility.title:
Regional routing
and constraints.
mobility.lead: Routing directly shapes experience quality. The Jozankei Ski Liner (guests only, ¥2,200 round trip, book online day before) lowers the barrier of winter driving and boosts long-stay regional value.
mobility.rentacarLink: Compare rental cars at New Chitose Airport
mobility.imgAlt: Asari Pass tunnel and winter highway—regional bottleneck

picks.title:
Curated
shortlist.
picks.lead: Stays, onsen, and dining chosen on five axes—snowscape, heritage, local food, onsen comfort, and access. A list you can build a regional loop from.

chaletIvy.name: Chalet Ivy Jozankei
chaletIvy.hook: 26 suite rooms with scenic baths—HNWI long-stay base for private snow-view soaking.

kuriyasuizan.name: Kuriyasuizan
kuriyasuizan.hook: Culinary inn inviting guests into the kitchen—13 rooms fusing Hokkaido produce and Japanese craft.

yurakusoan.name: Jozankei Yurakusoan
yurakusoan.hook: Natural hot spring in every room plus four free private baths—structural answer to shared-bath and tattoo concerns.

unwind.name: UNWIND HOTEL & BAR Otaru
unwind.hook: 1931 former foreign hotel renovated—Art Deco anchor for heritage tourism.

omo5.name: OMO5 Otaru by Hoshino Resorts
omo5.hook: Trusted Hoshino brand—3 min walk to Otaru Canal for post-ski strolls.

hoheikyo.name: Hoheikyo Onsen
hoheikyo.hook: 100% spring-fed open-air baths and cult-favorite Indian curry—immersive snowscape.

koganeyu.name: Koganeyu
koganeyu.hook: Private baths plus tattoo cover-sticker trial—ideal stop on regional loops.

garaku.name: Soup Curry GARAKU Sapporo
garaku.hook: Iconic Sapporo soul food—umami dashi meets secret spice blend.

masazushi.name: Masazushi Otaru Main
masazushi.hook: Sushi Street landmark—English menu, wheelchair access, vegetarian by reservation.

biergarten.name: Sapporo Beer Garden (Kaitakushi-kan)
biergarten.hook: Red-brick hall and Genghis Khan—frontier-era authenticity.

amenoyuki.name: Ame no Hi to Yuki no Hi
amenoyuki.hook: Café framing snow views—strong visual hook for millennials and Gen Z.

guidesTeasers.title: Stays · onsen · dining — full lists inside
guidesTeasers.lead: Venue details, phones, official links, and maps live on dedicated guide pages.

stay.label: Stays
stay.title: 5 curated stays
stay.hook: From Jozankei luxury to Otaru heritage hotels.

onsen.label: Onsen
onsen.title: 2 curated hot springs
onsen.hook: Snow-view rotenburo vs private baths—compare guest policies.

food.label: Dining
food.title: 4 curated restaurants
food.hook: Masazushi, GARAKU, beer hall—food that drives circulation.

tour.title:
Daytime circuit,
nights within each area.
tour.lead: Turn night closure into an asset: morning Japow → sunset Otaru sushi → Jozankei snow-view bath—via multi-base stays or rich in-area night content.

02.title: Jozankei Ski Liner
02.body: Guest bus avoids winter driving (book day before)

04.title: Jozankei · Yurakusoan
04.body: Private bath, snow views without culture friction

05.title: Sapporo · GARAKU
05.body: Next day: soup curry and Beer Garden in the city

triangle.title: Complete Golden Triangle guide
triangle.body: Link Sapporo, Jozankei, and Otaru through a ski hub. Jozankei Ski Liner as spine—Japow, gastronomy, onsen, and heritage in one journey.

mobility.title: Winter night closure and rescheduled routes
mobility.body: Asari Pass closed 19:00–07:00 blocks night circulation. Early sunset dinners, split Otaru/Jozankei bases, or richer in-area night content—schedules that match infrastructure reality.

culture.title: Tattoos, diet diversity—raising acceptance standards
culture.body: Yurakusoan private baths, Koganeyu cover stickers, Masazushi vegetarian prep—lift individual efforts to area norms. Multilingual visibility at booking is the competitiveness lever.

access.note: ~60 min from central Sapporo by bus or car · ~40–50 min from Otaru · ~30–40 min from Jozankei Onsen · parking available
access.places: 北海道Hokkaido札幌市Sapporo南区Minami-ku定山渓Jozankei937

four faces.

garaku.title: Soup Curry GARAKU

beer-garden.title: Sapporo Beer Garden

ame-yuki.title: Ame no Hi to Yuki no Hi

The stay itself
